svg.text: Enterprise Data Lake Platform — Corrected Architecture and Data Flow
v2.0 · Connector framework shared across ALL sources · Lake stages are STRICTLY SEQUENTIAL · Control plane roles clearly labeled
② EXTRACTION PIPELINE (runs per entity per schedule)
IAM DB auth or Secrets Mgr
Connector Runtime (Python 3.14) — Same framework for ALL sources. Only execution adapter differs per source.
SHARED FRAMEWORK — applies identically to Salesforce, NetSuite, MySQL and all future connectors
PER-SOURCE ADAPTERS — execution mechanism only differs here; shared framework above is identical for all
IAM DB auth or Secrets Mgr
③ Raw S3 Layer — Immutable Source-Aligned Storage (no transforms ever)
Format: JSON lines or Parquet (configured per entity)
NO transformation — stored exactly as received from source
Replay source for all downstream pipelines
Saves current field list to Schema Snapshot Store (S3)
Next run compares to detect: added / removed / changed fields
Drift report sent to CloudWatch + governance catalog
Defines start point for the NEXT incremental run window
On failure: no watermark advance · DLQ · alert · safe to replay
On Failure → no schema save · no watermark advance · route to DLQ · CloudWatch alarm
Step Functions error state → SQS DLQ capture → replay re-runs exact window safely
③→⑧ LAKE PROCESSING — STAGES ARE STRICTLY SEQUENTIAL (follow numbered order)
④ Transformation Jobs (AWS Glue / Spark) · Reads from Raw · Writes to Curated S3
▸ Null handling and default rules per field
▸ Reads Raw S3 → writes new Curated S3 files
▸ Maps source fields → canonical domain field
Canonical model applied here — NOT in extraction
⑤ Curated Layer — S3 · Standardized + Mapped + Quality-Checked
▸ Standardized data aligned to canonical domain model (all source fields translated to common names + types)
▸ Quality-checked records with DQ pass/fail markers · Path: /curated/{domain}/{entity}/{year}/{month}/ · Format: Parquet
▸ Separate S3 bucket from Raw with separate IAM policy · Entity resolution reads from here — never directly from Raw
Also: target DB loader can read directly from Curated for datasets that do not require entity resolution
Curated is the ONLY input to entity resolution — prevents resolution operating on un-standardized data
⑥ Entity Resolution Engine · Matches same real-world entity across all sources
▸ Cross-source: SF Account ↔ NS Customer ↔ MySQL
Reads from Curated only — never from Raw
▸ Defines which source field value wins per attribute
▸ e.g. NS wins for financials, SF wins for CRM fields
All matches traceable to Curated and Raw records
⑦ Golden Record Publisher · Single trusted master entity per customer/record
▸ One trusted master entity assembled from matched records across all sources (e.g. SF Account + NS Customer + MySQL user)
▸ output_fields in survivorship policy = explicit output schema; only declared fields appear in Parquet (no source-internal IDs)
▸ Entry point: GoldenRecordPublisher.from_registry(registry, entity_type) — no rules hardcoded in Lambda handler
This is what BI and AI/ML should consume — not raw or individual source curated records
Reproducible: same rules + same curated data → same golden record
Traceable: every field links back through curated to the raw source record
⑧ Analytics Layer — S3 · Consumption-Optimized · Fed by BOTH Curated data AND Golden Records (two converging paths)
▸ Parquet / Delta format — columnar, partitioned for fast queries
▸ Pre-aggregated and denormalized views for reporting
Queryable via Athena, Redshift Spectrum, Spark
▸ Both paths converge here — both available for analytics
▸ Target Database (RDS / Redshift / Lakehouse) ← loaded from here
▸ AI/ML feature pipelines and training datasets
Read-only IAM scopes enforced for all consumers
OBSERVABILITY, GOVERNANCE AND SECURITY (Cross-Cutting — every stage above emits telemetry here)
No PII · No credentials · Structured JSON only
Full trace: trigger → raw → curated → analytics
Change history for config + pipeline definitions
A01 Broken Access Control · A02 Misconfiguration
A03 Supply Chain · A04 Crypto · A05 Injection
SAST + SCA + Container + IaC policy scans in CI/CD
Least privilege IAM · no wildcard actions or resources
S3 lifecycle policies per classification tier
Data steward ownership per domain entity
TERRAFORM-MANAGED PLATFORM FOUNDATION — All infrastructure above is provisioned exclusively via Terraform (zero manual changes)
Modules: VPC · Subnets · IAM · S3+lifecycle · KMS · DynamoDB · Step Functions · Glue · Secrets Manager · CloudWatch · EventBridge · SQS · version-pinned · remote state in S3 + DynamoDB lock
AWS-first delivery · module contracts portable to Azure and GCP · policy-as-code (OPA/Sentinel) in CI/CD · root + child module hierarchy · approved modules in private registry
Green boxes = shared framework (identical for ALL sources) · Purple boxes = per-source execution adapters · Numbered ③→⑧ = strictly sequential pipeline

Watermark advances ONLY after full success · Raw data is NEVER transformed in-place · Curated and Analytics are separate S3 buckets with separate IAM policies · Golden Records are traceable to source records in Raw
